item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get that the whole truth serum spray is making your characters talk funny, but the interview made me cringe multiple times with how forced some of the dialogue comes across as, especially lines like these:

Davey, for God's sake! If we ask about Kephart then we'll figure that out naturally. Also, that's really rude and somewhat unprofessional! We've prepared a series of questions.

Oh nothing, I just sort of let something classified slip against my will because thanks Kephart. These guys also make us mad.

No really, it's fine. We've been feeling some attraction since we started spending day in and day out together researching a company that doesn't exist. But it's not like we're a couple or anything.

On top of that, the anomaly just doesn't really do much for me. I feel a bit of sympathy for the staff, but ultimately there is just not enough going on here to really draw me in, which is a shame since at its core truth serum vacation seems like it would be a fertile ground for an SCP article.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Back: SCP-3643 | Next: SCP-3645

Let's see what you can do with 'it was a dark and stormy night…'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the premise; a bunch of fish-headed 'servants' assigned to an island and left behind to serve at the whims of their 'guests' is intriguing enough. There's a couple of things holding me back from an upvote, though — there's a few grammatical hiccups and a lot of tightening that needs to be done in that interview. I'm too tired to really go through the interview, but I gave some examples below of places where I think the opening bits can be tightened:

FEEDBACK
– hide block

Info taken from interviews with the general manager of SCP-3644 indicates little oversight from GoI-4889; therefore, SCP-3644's beneficial properties mean that it is suitable as an approved vacation destination for Foundation personnel Levels 2-4.

The effects of SCP-3644-2 means that personnel should not stay longer than five days to avoid overexposure, and any personnel with access to classified information is to undergo a small amnestic regimen, and be briefed on their duties upon return. The general manager of SCP-3644 has agreed to restrict bookings to Foundation employees only,

To:

Foundation personnel are not to stay longer than five days. Upon their return, personnel are to undergo a low-dose amnestic regimen and be rebriefed on their duties. The general manager of SCP-3644 has agreed to restrict bookings to Foundation employees only.

All SCP-3646-1 instances may not leave the grounds of SCP-3644 due to their condition, but are allowed to stay at their homes within the grounds. With the help of the general manager, the SCP-3643 containment team, now the primary team assigned to GoI-4889, has built a Foundation operated store that provides amenities to the SCP-3644-1 instances so that they may be better served in their operation of SCP-3646.

To:

All SCP-3646-1 instances are prohibited from leaving SCP-3644. A Foundation operated store is to provide amenities to these instances so that they may be better served in their operation of SCP-3646.

It is wholly owned by GoI-4889 ("Kephart Corporation"), an unknown corporate entity of uncertain goals currently on negative terms with the Foundation.

To:

It is owned by GoI-4889 ("Kephart Corporation"), a corporate entity currently on negative terms with the Foundation.

The main anomaly of SCP-3644 can be divided into two main components: SCP-3644-1 and SCP-3644-2.

SCP-3644-1 instances have no difficulty talking, eating, or carrying out tasks as staff despite their anomaly, and are employed in a wide variety of jobs throughout SCP-3644 — ranging from janitor to DJ to marine biologist.

Due to the difficulty in analyzing SCP-3644-2's liquid form, its chemical makeup is not yet fully understood.

(possessive "its" doesn't get the apostrophe; only the conjunction (it is) does)

Also, there's a few things I don't get, and a few things I think you should either change or tweak.

QUESTIONS
– hide block
  • Why does the Foundation open a store for the instances? Why not just give them food? I presumed there was some anomalous feature here that made them forced to buy their food (kind of like a company store in a mining town), but that didn't turn out to be the case.
  • The decision to have them be genetically related to aboriginal groups seems… I don't know. Risky? I'd consider taking that part out for possible unintended implications: On one hand, this is a skip about capitalism exploiting a native population; on the other hand, it's also a skip about a native population literally created as servants (and ultimately serving to provide recreational services to the Foundation). Like, there's some possibly unintentional Bad Thingstm you might be implying, here. IDK, though.
  • The effect of the spray on the instances seems unnecessary; like, this anomalous corporation constructed them. I'd consider either getting rid of the spray's effect on the instances (and just making it so the instances were created to be incapable of doing these things), or have the spray be a pheromone the instances create (in which case, definitely don't make them actual aboriginal populations — because this would then literally be a species created to make rich tourists happy. Which strikes me as an interesting theme, but not if you connect it to actual aboriginal groups).

Anyway, that's it — I think the premise here is pretty solid, but the interview felt like it meandered a bit, and certain elements left me confused — that being said, I think that there's a pretty solid skip here with some work/changes.

Good luck with the contest!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Part of the 72-hour Jam Contest, day two.

Part Two of The Kephart Trilogy.

Photo by my family a few years back, used with permission

Special thanks to Jean Michel Cousteau and his eco-resort, with apologies.
